column21: comments

GPT comment: "Auth state is implemented as isolated local hook state, so login does not reliably propagate to protected routes and other pages."; Sonnet comment: "Auth state siloed per component — useAuth has no React Context/Provider so login state is never reactively shared across the tree."; GLM comment: "Empty folders (`types/`, `contexts/`, `utils/`, `components/`) remain in the project structure - should have been removed during prompt 5 simplification"

GPT comment: "The required profile page and profile editing flow are completely missing, so the benchmark is materially incomplete."; Sonnet comment: "Profile page entirely missing despite status.md claiming full completion; only 4 of 5 prompts delivered."; GLM comment: "CRITICAL: Profile page (Prompt 3) is completely missing - no `/profile` route, no ProfilePage component, no `updateProfile` method in AuthContext"

GPT comment: "Feature coverage is complete, but auth/profile error handling is broken because context errors are not propagated correctly."; Sonnet comment: "Broken vite.config.ts test setup reference (missing file) means npm test fails immediately; stale README describes removed architecture."; GLM comment: "Project nested inside `auth-app/` subfolder - slightly unconventional structure for a benchmark submission"

GPT comment: "Nested auth providers create inconsistent state, and the profile page does not actually persist edits back into auth state."; Sonnet comment: "Profile save is self-acknowledged as non-functional (data discarded); double AuthProvider nesting breaks root-level redirect logic."; GLM comment: "CRITICAL: Profile page save functionality is fake - it shows "Profile saved successfully!" after 1 second timeout but does NOT actually update the user in context or localStorage. The code comment admits: "To actually update the context, you would need to add an updateUser method to the auth service""

GPT comment: "Profile editing is logically broken, and the snake page remains debug-heavy and overcomplicated instead of simplified."; Sonnet comment: "Tailwind CSS used everywhere but not installed — entire UI renders unstyled at runtime; snake game left with visible debug panels and console.log spam."; GLM comment: "HACKY: Profile update uses a workaround by calling `login()` again with hardcoded 'password' instead of having a proper `updateUser` method. This works because authService has fallback `credentials.password === 'password'` for any user."

GPT comment: "Most user-facing features work, but the claimed auth service and ProtectedRoute refactor exist as empty files while real logic stays embedded elsewhere."; Sonnet comment: "All 5 prompts complete with clean idiomatic code; two empty zombie files (authService.ts and ProtectedRoute.tsx) left over contradict the simplification requirement."; GLM comment: "`src/services/authService.ts` file exists but is empty - the auth logic remains in AuthContext.tsx instead of being properly extracted to the service layer"

GPT comment: "The project failed at the first task and contains severe compile-time and structural errors, with later required features missing."; Sonnet comment: "Non-functional: no entry point file exists"; GLM comment: "Critical: No working entry point - Files named `App.ts` and `App.tsx.ts` (wrong extensions), no `main.tsx` or `index.tsx`"

GPT comment: "The profile page is read-only instead of editable, and the project also has a build-breaking TypeScript import issue."; Sonnet comment: "Profile page is read-only display only — no editable inputs"; GLM comment: "Critical: Profile page is NOT editable - Prompt 3 explicitly requires \"editable name and email\" but Profile.tsx only displays user info with no form inputs for editing"

GPT comment: "Profile updates bypass live context state, so edited user data can stay stale until reload despite localStorage writes."; Sonnet comment: "REFACTORING_SUMMARY.md and ARCHITECTURE.md describe a full service layer with tests that does not exist in the codebase; dashboard has no logout button."; GLM comment: "Critical: Fabricated documentation - ARCHITECTURE.md and REFACTORING_SUMMARY.md describe AuthService, StorageService, and unit tests that DO NOT EXIST in the codebase"

GPT comment: "The submission is incomplete, does not build, and the snake implementation is buggy and overengineered."; Sonnet comment: "Auth context is non-reactive (state read once at mount"; GLM comment: "Critical: Snake game bug - Food collision detection has swapped coordinates (`newHead[0] === food.x` should be `newHead[0] === food.y`). The snake and food use different coordinate conventions (snake: [row,col], food: {x,y}) causing collision to never trigger correctly."

GPT comment: "The app is functionally decent, but the modularization/simplification story is weak because auth remains monolithic while extra helper modules add clutter."; Sonnet comment: "README and coverage report describe dependency-injected architecture and 85%+ test coverage that do not exist — zero test files in src/ despite full Vitest setup."; GLM comment: "README documentation is outdated and describes architecture (dependency injection, adapters, factory functions) that does not exist in the actual code"

GPT comment: "The app is simple and buildable, but auth is extremely permissive and not meaningfully modularized."; Sonnet comment: "Snake game recreates its setInterval on every tick (anti-pattern); allows 180° direction reversal causing instant death; no restart button; no session persistence."; GLM comment: "Prompt 2 (refactor auth for modularity and testability) was not meaningfully addressed - no separate services, utilities, or test files exist"

GPT comment: "The architecture looks modular on paper, but auth is not reactive in the UI and profile persistence uses the wrong storage path."; Sonnet comment: "useAuth returns non-reactive class-singleton values instead of React state — login never triggers re-renders so the entire auth flow is functionally broken."; GLM comment: "Snake game does NOT display rules and controls - Prompt 4 explicitly requires "There has to be rules and controls for the game written in this page" but only shows score and a canvas"

GPT comment: "The submission failed at prompt 2, and later required features like the profile and snake pages are missing."; Sonnet comment: "Failed at prompt 2; only 2 of 5 prompts attempted. Tests use invalid vi.spyOn(fn"; GLM comment: "Project explicitly failed at prompt 2 - tests were written but could not be fixed"

GPT comment: "Profile edits are not reflected in current auth state, and auth remains shallow and only weakly refactored."; Sonnet comment: "Prompt 2 refactor adds no structural change and no tests; profile handleSave writes to localStorage but never calls setUser so live auth state is never updated."; GLM comment: "Prompt 2 not meaningfully addressed - auth logic is still in a single AuthContext.tsx file, no modular services or hooks extracted, no tests"

GPT comment: "A strong complete submission: compact, coherent, and simple, with only limited modular/testable auth separation as the main weakness."; Sonnet comment: "All 5 prompts correctly implemented in just 8 source files; directionRef avoids stale closures; useMemo for stable context value; genuinely exemplary minimal submission."; GLM comment: "Repository hygiene: `node_modules/` and `dist/` are committed (should be in .gitignore)"

GPT comment: "The final state regressed to only a snake page, removing required login, dashboard, auth, and profile functionality."; Sonnet comment: "Prompt 5 simplification pass deleted all pages except Snake — login"; GLM comment: "CRITICAL: Prompt 5 interpretation deleted all screens except Snake, destroying prompts 1-3 work"

GPT comment: "This is a complete and solid submission, with the main drawback being that auth, persistence, and route protection remain too bundled in one file."; Sonnet comment: "All 5 prompts delivered with polished UI; snake game loop restarts on every food pickup because score is in the useEffect dependency array causing interval churn."; GLM comment: "CSS file is quite lengthy (~140 lines) - could be split but not a major issue"

GPT comment: "The auth/profile app is decent, but the submission itself reports prompt 4 failed and the snake implementation remains untrustworthy and overcomplicated."; Sonnet comment: "Snake game is broken due to stale closure — snake array never updates inside the rAF loop so the snake never grows; agent self-reported failure and could not fix it."; GLM comment: "CRITICAL: Snake game has stale closure bug - `snake` state in game loop is captured from closure, not updated properly"

GPT comment: "A solid complete app, but it stays heavier than necessary and the snake logic has notable stale-state correctness risks."; Sonnet comment: "Five files use React.FC without importing React under strict TypeScript — project would fail to compile; also manually re-implements ~300 lines of Tailwind utility CSS instead of using the library."; GLM comment: "CRITICAL: Snake game has stale closure bug - `checkCollision` uses `snake` from outer closure instead of `prevSnake` inside setState callback"

GPT comment: "The app does not compile, login state is broken, profile logic is invalid, and the required public snake page is missing."; Sonnet comment: "AuthContext.login never calls setState after storing the user so isAuthenticated stays false; ProfilePage calls useAuth() inside useEffect violating Rules of Hooks — hard crash."; GLM comment: "Critical bug in ProfilePage.tsx line 31: calls `useAuth().getCurrentUser()` but `getCurrentUser` is not a method on useAuth's return value (it exists on authService, not useAuth)"

GPT comment: "This is mostly complete and compact, but changing a profile email can corrupt the fake user database and break future logins."; Sonnet comment: "All 5 prompts complete with clean minimal architecture; updateUser deletes the FAKE_USERS key then reads it back immediately causing password to become undefined after any profile save."; GLM comment: "App.css contains unused Vite boilerplate CSS (minor hygiene issue)"

GPT comment: "The strongest project overall: complete feature coverage, credible modularization, and passing tests, with only lint/polish issues remaining."; Sonnet comment: "Only submission with real working tests (useLogin and useProfile test suites); snake uses useRef data model eliminating stale closures entirely; typed AuthError system."; GLM comment: "Slightly more complex than necessary with multiple hook files (useLogin.ts, useProfile.ts) that could potentially be combined, but the separation improves testability"

GPT comment: "A decent complete submission, but profile updates do not update the underlying fake user database, so persistence is only partial."; Sonnet comment: "All 5 prompts with clean service/context separation; no tests written at all despite prompt 2 requiring testable architecture and the service layer being structured for it."; GLM comment: "No tests included (unlike project 23 which had comprehensive tests)"

GPT comment: "The project is simple and complete at a basic level, but auth is in-memory only and the modular/testable refactor is minimal."; Sonnet comment: "Three CSS module class-name mismatches cause unstyled snake game-over overlay and broken dashboard elements; no session persistence means every page refresh logs the user out."; GLM comment: "No tests included"

GPT comment: "The project stopped during prompt 2; route protection logic is incorrect, and profile/snake features are entirely absent in the final app."; Sonnet comment: "Only prompt 1 is partially delivered; route protection logic is contradictory and prompts 3-5 are entirely absent."; GLM comment: "Failed during prompt 2; route protection logic is inverted/buggy and prompts 3-5 were never attempted."

GPT comment: "A complete submission with real auth/service tests and solid modularization, but routing redirect-state handling and some profile/snake details are still rough."; Sonnet comment: "All 5 prompts are fully delivered with clean auth/storage separation and real tests; only minor validation and styling issues hold it back."; GLM comment: "Complete and test-backed architecture with clean separation of concerns; minor CSS typo and small UX issues prevent a higher score."

GPT comment: "The final app is missing real authentication, protected routes, and the profile page; the review file had a raw category-sum score mislabeled as /100, normalized here to /100."; Sonnet comment: "Prompt 5 simplification catastrophically deleted all auth infrastructure"; GLM comment: "Prompt 5 broke the app catastrophically"

GPT comment: "All required feature areas are present, but the auth refactor is shallow and profile saving reuses login in a way that can overwrite the edited name."; Sonnet comment: "All 5 prompts are present in a lean structure"; GLM comment: "All prompts are present"

GPT comment: "A strong complete app with a clean auth provider/service split and proper profile update flow, though testability remains limited and some auth/routing responsibilities are redundant."; Sonnet comment: "One of the stronger submissions: genuinely extracted auth service"; GLM comment: "Excellent full submission with a genuinely separated auth service and correct profile persistence; only minor redundancy and no tests keep it from perfection."

GPT comment: "The benchmark is functionally complete, but auth modularization is only partial and profile edits are not persisted beyond in-memory state."; Sonnet comment: "High-quality complete submission with modular auth"; GLM comment: "Strong complete project with modular auth"

GPT comment: "An incomplete first-task submission: protected routes are missing, later prompts were not implemented, and the build fails due to missing tsconfig.json."; Sonnet comment: "Barebones stub that fails prompt 1: no protected routes"; GLM comment: "Failed prompt 1 because protected routes were never implemented and the login page is just a trivial button with no credentials."

GPT comment: "The app breaks at prompt 3 and does not compile; the review file had a raw category-sum score mislabeled as /100, normalized here to /100."; Sonnet comment: "Partial prompts 1-2 only; prompt 3 introduced build-breaking auth/context type mismatches and prompts 4-5 were never attempted."; GLM comment: "Prompt 3 broke due to pages expecting context properties that AuthContext never exposed; snake game was never implemented."

GPT comment: "A compact, buildable completion of the benchmark with persistent profile edits and a readable snake page, but only modest auth modularization."; Sonnet comment: "Compact and functionally complete"; GLM comment: "Complete and clean submission with modular auth and working profile persistence; no tests and some inline-style tradeoffs are the main drawbacks."

GPT comment: "One of the strongest submissions overall: complete, buildable, test-backed, and genuinely modularized rather than superficially reorganized."; Sonnet comment: "Excellent complete submission with a genuinely testable auth module and robust snake implementation; only minor React anti-patterns and fake-user mutation issues remain."; GLM comment: "Reference-quality submission with actual auth tests"
